name: Ground Zero
$comment: The docs/23 demo as ONE definition. What sets this world apart from the Space scene is not the drop (Space drops a moon too); it is the ground block: ground zero declared as a lat/lon site ON the shared Earth (ground.planet names assets/bodies/earth.json, the body the orbital cast places), with the iron ball as declared cohesive matter standing at the impact site, the witness the zoom milestones descend to. The system half is what runs today: the space band loads this file and you can brake or drop Luna into the Earth. The definition-driven Simulation already builds and runs the ground half natively; nothing renders the ball at orbital scale yet. The zoom milestones (docs/59 order of work 2 to 4: descent camera, conserved hand-down, re-coherence) consume this block to materialize ground zero when the camera commits, so the drop seen from space and the ball's fate on the ground become one event read from one file.
